per_question.question: What are the standard office hours for the center?
per_question.ground_truth: Standard office hours are Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM.
per_question.answer:
According to Context [Context 3], the standard office hours for the Career and Advising Center are:

"Please note changes to hours of operation during the summer, holidays, and days with no scheduled classes."

However, it is specified that during the school year, the center can be found in Ceres Hall 306 from 8 am to 5 pm.

per_question.question: How do office hours change during the summer and break periods?
per_question.ground_truth: Summer and break hours are Monday through Friday, 7:30 AM to 4:00 PM.

TH Thursday 8:00AM - 5:00PM

per_question.question: What is the primary career management tool used at NDSU for jobs and internships?
per_question.ground_truth: The primary career management tool is Handshake.
per_question.answer:
According to the provided context, the primary career management tool used at NDSU for jobs and internships is Handshake. This is mentioned in both Context 1 and Context 2.

Context 1: "Handshake is the official North Dakota State University student and alumni job board, posting information about current on-campus employment, including work-study and internships, both part-time and full-time positions for graduating students, or graduate assistantship opportunities."

Context 2: "Handshake : This is the first place we encourage students to look for opportunities. Handshake was specifically created for college students. Employers turn to this job board first in order to find student talent. Login with your NDSU student credentials. (NDSU Alumni have access to Handshake as well!)"

Source:
https://career-advising.ndsu.edu/channels/career-support/posts/page/19/
https://career-advising.ndsu.edu/resources/how-to-find-an-internship/

per_question.question: What must incoming students do before creating a Handshake account?
per_question.ground_truth: Incoming students must claim their NDSU email Bison Credentials first.
per_question.answer:
According to the provided context, incoming students must "Claim your NDSU Email (Bison Credentials) here" before creating a Handshake account. [Context 1]

Source:
https://career-advising.ndsu.edu/handshake/

per_question.question: Which email domain should students use to log into Handshake, according to the NDSU guidance note?
per_question.ground_truth: Students should use @ndsu.edu, not ndus.edu.
per_question.answer:
According to the provided context, students should use "@ndsu.edu" to log into Handshake.

Source:
[Context 1]
https://career-advising.ndsu.edu/handshake/

per_question.question: What types of opportunities are listed in Handshake according to the NDSU career site?
per_question.ground_truth: Part-time, full-time, work-study, volunteer, graduate assistantship, and internship opportunities are listed in Handshake.
per_question.answer:
According to the provided context, Handshake is a platform where students can post their online profile and apply for internships and other opportunities. Employers can post open positions that students can apply to. The types of opportunities listed in Handshake include all types of jobs and internships for students and alumni to see and apply to.

Source: [Context 3] Ask a CM! – Career and Advising Center – North Dakota State University

per_question.question: How is Handshake described on NDSU career resources pages?
per_question.ground_truth: Handshake is described as the number one place students find jobs and internships.
per_question.answer:
According to the provided context, Handshake is described as an "online platform" that is introduced as a resource for incoming students this fall. It is mentioned in the article titled "Welcome to NDSU!" under the section "Handshake Handshake is an online platform that allows you to search and apply for internships, jobs, and graduate school opportunities."

Source:
https://career-advising.ndsu.edu/channels/explore-majors-careers-opportunities/posts/page/1/
https://career-advising.ndsu.edu/videos/creating-an-employer-account-for-ndsu-faculty-and-staff/

per_question.question: How do employers register for the H.E.L.P.S. Career Fair?
per_question.ground_truth: Employers register by emailing the Events Manager directly.
per_question.answer:

per_question.question: What is the minimum cumulative GPA required to register an internship for academic credit?
per_question.ground_truth: The minimum cumulative GPA required is 2.0.

per_question.question: How many work hours are required in a semester to maintain full-time status while registered for 3 internship credits?
per_question.ground_truth: A minimum of 300 work hours is required.
per_question.answer:
According to the context, students can maintain a full-time student status by registering for 3 credits through the NDSU Career and Advising Center or by working 300+ hours in a given semester.

Source:
https://career-advising.ndsu.edu/blog/2019/10/25/ask-a-cm-4/

per_question.question: What is the primary career management tool used at NDSU for jobs and internships?
per_question.ground_truth: The primary career management tool is Handshake.
per_question.trust_answer_raw:
According to the provided context, the primary career management tool used at NDSU for jobs and internships is Handshake. This is mentioned in both Context 1 and Context 2.

Context 1: "Handshake is the official North Dakota State University student and alumni job board, posting information about current on-campus employment, including work-study and internships, both part-time and full-time positions for graduating students, or graduate assistantship opportunities."

Context 2: "Handshake : This is the first place we encourage students to look for opportunities. Handshake was specifically created for college students. Employers turn to this job board first in order to find student talent. Login with your NDSU student credentials. (NDSU Alumni have access to Handshake as well!)"

Source:
https://career-advising.ndsu.edu/channels/career-support/posts/page/19/
https://career-advising.ndsu.edu/resources/how-to-find-an-internship/
